rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE WILSON et al VS. 1049 MARKET STREET, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant 1049 Market Street, Llc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication As To The Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Asserted Within Plaintiffs Complaint As Well As Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015 line 1. Defendant 1049 Market Street, Llc'S Motion For Summary Adjudication As To The Second, Third And Fourth Causes Of Action Asserted Within Plaintiffs Complaint As Well As Plaintiffs Claim For Punitive Damages Hearing Required. = (501/GLW)D

rulings.Case Title: HERO CHIANG et al VS. NADER HEYDAYIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Nader Heydayian, Scott Pelichoff And Janet Brayers Notice Of Special Motion To Strike Civil General: Amended Causes Of Action As A Slapp Suit And For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015 line 2. DEFENDANTS NADER HEYDAYIAN, JANET BRAYER and SCOTT PELICHOFF'sf Special Motion To Strike Civil General: Amended Causes Of Action As A Slapp Suit And For Attorneys Fees And Costs GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to Janet Brayer. All causes of action alleged as to Janet Brayer (Amended Causes of Action 4:14-5-14) arise out of protected activity. Plaintiff failed to show probability of prevailing on the merits. Motion is DENIED as to Scott Pelichoff and Nader Heydayian, the gravamen of the causes of action alleged as to the co-owner landlords is not protected activity, i.e. "creating a false scene of "BUILDING UNSAFE," "paying money to hire officers front he Fire Department to get new REPORTS," "going around [unlawful detainer procedure] without filing any document at the Court and without obtaining a Writ of Possession," "hiring for stalking," and remodeling the units. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: HERO CHIANG et al VS. NADER HEYDAYIAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015 line 2. DEFENDANTS NADER HEYDAYIAN, JANET BRAYER and SCOTT PELICHOFF's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT SUSTAINED. Demurrer as to causes of action alleged against Janet Brayer is moot in light of the ruling on the CCP 425.16 motion. Demurrer to the sole cause of action for deceit as to Scott Pelichoff is sustained without leave to amend as it is barred by the 3-year Statute of Limitations (misrepresentations made in February 2011, Complaint filed on June 25, 2014). Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to Nader Heydayian for Plaintiffs to allege each cause of action separately and to allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: HERO CHIANG et al VS. NADER HEYDAYIAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Oppose Defendants' Proposed Order Dated June 4th; And Motion To Continue Motion To Disclaim Greatwalls Chiang
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015 line 2. PLAINTIFFS HERO CHIANG and JANEVA CHIANG's Motion To Oppose Defendants' Proposed Order Dated June 4th; And Motion To Continue Motion To Disclaim Greatwalls Chiang DENIED. On June 3, 2015 the Court adopted its tentative ruling to take the motion to disclaim off calendar for insufficient notice. No appearance by the parties. The Court did not receive a proposed order dated June 4, 2015. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA ALEXANDREA VS. ANTOINE J. WATTS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015 line 3. DEFENDANT ANTOINE WATTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OFF CALENDAR, dismissal filed on July 2, 2015. = (501/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY MUNN VS. ANN LEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, July 7, 2015 line 4. DEFENDANT ANN LEE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges licensor-licensee agreement and no facts suggesting existence of tenancy are alleged, such as exclusive possession or payment of rent. See Van Goerlitz v. Turner (1944) 65 Cal.App.2d 425; Spinks v. Equity Residential Briarwood Apartments (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1004. Defendant and her counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6C in future. = (501/GLW)
